meaning: adj.虚弱的,衰弱的,无力的,微弱的,薄弱的
examples: The heartbeat was feeble and irregular.
Don't be so feeble! Tell her you don't want to go.
At last a feeble voice replied, "Thank you so much, dear Ratty!"
Mr Bush's proposed budget cuts are pretty feeble, but they might set that ball rolling.
Wal-Mart had a particularly feeble month and expects sales to be flat in November.
